events.text: Every machine connection begins with a configuration profile -- it holds SSH keys, server addresses, and infrastructure settings. We create a new profile now, linked to your private key.
Profile created. We verify it appears in the configuration list alongside any existing profiles, showing their adapter type and registered machine count.
To reach a remote server, we register it by IP address and SSH user. The CLI also fetches the server's host key fingerprint to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
Machine registered. We confirm the entry is stored correctly. You'll see all machines in this profile with their IP addresses and SSH connection details.
When managing a single server, a default machine eliminates repetitive targeting flags. Every command will route to this machine unless you override it explicitly.
Before deploying anything, we verify the SSH tunnel is functional. A remote hostname check proves the machine is reachable and the host key is trusted.
